message:
🌱 Add vllm: [Feature]: Support "required" option in tool_choice mission (#853)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "vllm-13002-feature-support-required-option-in-tool-choice",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "vllm: Support \"required\" option in tool_choice",
+    "description": "Add support for the \"required\" option in the tool_choice parameter of the OpenAI-compatible chat completions API. When tool_choice is set to \"required\", the model must call at least one tool in its response. This is critical for agent-based workflows. Requested by 25+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current vLLM setup",
+        "description": "Verify your vLLM version and configuration:\n```bash\npip show vllm | grep Version\n# or\npython -c \"import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)\"\n```\nThe `tool_choice: \"required\"` option requires a recent version of vLLM with tool calling support."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand tool_choice options",
+        "description": "The OpenAI API supports several `tool_choice` values:\n- `\"auto\"` - The model decides whether to call a tool (already supported)\n- `\"none\"` - The model will not call any tool (already supported)\n- `\"required\"` - The model must call at least one tool (this feature)\n- `{\"type\": \"function\", \"function\": {\"name\": \"...\"}}` - Force a specific tool\n\nThe `\"required\"` option is essential for agentic workflows where every response must include a tool call."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Start vLLM with tool calling enabled",
+        "description": "Launch the vLLM server with a model that supports tool calling:\n```bash\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \\\n  --model <model-with-tool-support> \\\n  --enable-auto-tool-choice \\\n  --tool-call-parser hermes\n```\nThe `--enable-auto-tool-choice` and `--tool-call-parser` flags configure the tool calling behavior."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify tool_choice required works",
+        "description": "Test using `tool_choice: \"required\"` in a chat completion request:\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\n    \"model\": \"<model-name>\",\n    \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"What is the weather in Paris?\"}],\n    \"tools\": [{\"type\": \"function\", \"function\": {\"name\": \"get_weather\", \"parameters\": {\"type\": \"object\", \"properties\": {\"location\": {\"type\": \"string\"}}}}}],\n    \"tool_choice\": \"required\"\n  }'\n```\nThe response should always include a tool call to `get_weather`, regardless of the query content."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "vLLM added support for the \"required\" option in the tool_choice parameter. When set to \"required\", the model is constrained to always include at least one tool call in its response. This is critical for agentic AI workflows where tool calls are mandatory for task execution.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Start vLLM with tool calling\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \\\n  --model <model> \\\n  --enable-auto-tool-choice \\\n  --tool-call-parser hermes",
+        "# Use tool_choice: required\ncurl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"model\": \"<model>\", \"messages\": [...], \"tools\": [...], \"tool_choice\": \"required\"}'"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "vllm",
+      "community",
+      "llm-serving",
+      "feature",
+      "tool-calling",
+      "agents",
+      "function-calling"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "vllm"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/13002",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm"
+    },
+    "reactions": 25,
+    "comments": 4,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "python",
+      "pip"
+    ],
+    "description": "Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm) with a model that supports tool calling."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:45:54.956Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
